contract_threat_patterns.name: Unsigned Contract Amendments
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contract terms modified through informal channels without formal amendment process.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Vendor emails updated terms with modified liability cap buried in attachment. Customer does not notice the change and continues performance under amended terms.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for 'amendments must be in writing signed by authorised representatives' clause. Review email trail for informal term changes.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include robust amendment clause requiring written agreement by authorised representatives. Maintain contract change log.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when target involves third-party relationships or contract management processes.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Deliberate Scope Creep Manipulation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider incrementally expands or shifts scope without formal change control to increase billing.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider gradually performs out-of-scope work without raising change orders, then retrospectively invoices for 'additional services' citing implied acceptance.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Compare actual deliverables against contracted scope. Review invoices for items not in original scope.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Implement strict change control procedure. No work without approved change order. Regular scope reviews.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when scope definition is vague or change control process is absent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Data Hostage on Contract Termination
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider withholds customer data or makes extraction impractical to force contract renewal.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer terminates contract. Provider cites 'administrative processing time' to delay data return, then requires expensive professional services for data migration.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for data return/deletion clause with specific timeframe, format requirements, and penalty for non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include data return within 30 days in standard format. Add penalty for late return. Ensure ongoing API access during transition.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when data portability and exit provisions are weak or absent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: SLA Measurement Gaming
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider manipulates SLA measurement to report compliance despite actual service degradation.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider redefines 'planned maintenance' broadly to exclude most downtime from SLA calculations, achieving 99.9% on paper while actual availability is 98%.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review SLA measurement methodology and exclusions. Compare provider reports with customer-observed experience.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define measurement methodology precisely. Limit maintenance windows. Customer-side monitoring as verification.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when SLA measurement methodology is undefined or exclusions are broad.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Sub-Processor Control Circumvention
contract_threat_patterns.description: Processor engages sub-processors without proper notification or authorisation.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Cloud provider's infrastructure partner engages new sub-processor in non-adequate country. Original provider does not notify controller. Customer data flows to unapproved jurisdiction.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Regular sub-processor list audits. Compare actual data flows with approved sub-processor list.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require 30-day advance notification. Include audit rights over sub-processor chain. Contractual penalty for unauthorised sub-processing.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when data flows involve third parties or cloud infrastructure with subcontracting chains.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Deliberate Breach Notification Delay
contract_threat_patterns.description: Processor delays breach notification to investigate internally or manage PR before notifying controller.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider discovers data breach on Monday. Internal investigation takes 2 weeks. Provider notifies customer 18 days after discovery, missing the 72-hour regulatory deadline.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Include contractual requirement for notification within 24 hours of awareness. Verify incident logs during audits.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define 'awareness' trigger clearly. Require 24-hour notification. Include contractual penalty for late notification. Annual incident response testing.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when breach notification timeframe exceeds 48 hours or lacks specificity.

contract_threat_patterns.name: IP Ownership Ambiguity Exploitation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider claims ownership of deliverables despite customer funding, exploiting ambiguous IP clauses.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer funds bespoke software development. Contract has vague IP clause. Provider licenses same software to customer's competitor, claiming it is 'background IP'.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review IP clause for clear distinction between background and foreground IP. Check for defined IP schedule.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define background IP in schedule. Assign foreground IP explicitly. Include non-compete for customer-specific deliverables.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when IP ownership is ambiguous or background/foreground distinction is unclear.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Engineered Vendor Lock-In
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider deliberately creates switching barriers through proprietary formats, integration dependencies, and data formats.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider uses proprietary data format requiring conversion tools only they provide. Export feature is 'available' but outputs incomplete data missing critical metadata.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Test data export functionality during contract term. Verify completeness and usability of exported data.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require standard data formats (CSV, JSON, XML). Test export capability annually. Include data completeness warranty in export.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when data format is proprietary or export capability is untested.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Confidential Information Exploitation via Residuals Clause
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses broad residuals clause to justify use of customer's confidential information and trade secrets.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider's consultant works on customer's proprietary algorithm. Under residuals clause, consultant 'retains in unaided memory' the algorithm concept and applies it to competitor's project.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review residuals clause scope. Check for trade secret and personal data exclusions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Narrow residuals clause to general skills only. Exclude: trade secrets, personal data, specific designs, and proprietary algorithms.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when residuals clause is broad or covers trade secret information.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Price Manipulation Through Ambiguous Terms
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider exploits vague pricing language to introduce unexpected charges.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contract says 'standard rates apply for additional services'. Provider unilaterally defines 'standard rates' at 3x market rate for out-of-scope work that becomes necessary.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for fully defined pricing in all scenarios. Flag 'standard rates', 'reasonable charges', or 'as agreed' language.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define all pricing explicitly. Cap ad hoc rates. Require prior approval for any charges not in pricing schedule.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag vague pricing language without defined rates or caps.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Systematic Audit Obstruction
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses procedural tactics to prevent effective audits.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider requires 120-day audit notice, limits scope to pre-selected documents, restricts auditor access to sanitised environments, and challenges auditor qualifications.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review audit clause for: notice period reasonableness, scope completeness, auditor qualification, and access rights.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: 30-day notice maximum. Unrestricted scope for regulatory audits. Customer-chosen auditor (subject to NDA). Access to production systems.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when audit rights have excessive notice periods or restricted scope.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Force Majeure Clause Abuse
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider invokes force majeure for events within their control to avoid performance obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider claims IT infrastructure failure as force majeure event despite it being caused by their failure to maintain systems. Refuses service credits citing FM protection.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review force majeure definition. Ensure provider's own failures are excluded. Check for mitigation obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Narrowly define FM events. Exclude events within provider's reasonable control. Require mitigation efforts and regular status updates.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when force majeure definition is broad or lacks mitigation duties.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Data Loss from Provider Insolvency
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider insolvency results in loss of access to data and services with no recovery mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: SaaS provider enters administration. Administrators suspend all services. Customer data is on servers that may be sold to creditors. No escrow or export was arranged.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for insolvency provisions, data escrow, and source code escrow arrangements.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include: insolvency termination right (where enforceable), data escrow with independent agent, source code escrow, and regular data backup to customer.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when critical service has no insolvency protection or data escrow.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Contract Assignment to Hostile or Incompetent Party
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider assigns contract to party that is a competitor, sanctioned entity, or lacks capability.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider is acquired by customer's direct competitor. Contract is assigned to new parent. Competitor gains access to customer's confidential information and strategic plans.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for assignment restrictions and change of control provisions with consent requirements.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require prior written consent for all assignments. Treat change of control as assignment. Include termination right on change of control.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when assignment or change of control provisions are weak.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Gradual Data Transfer to Non-Adequate Jurisdiction
contract_threat_patterns.description: Data gradually migrates to non-adequate jurisdictions through infrastructure changes and sub-processor additions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider migrates analytics workload to US-based sub-processor. Then moves backups to Singapore. Neither change triggers notification as each is 'minor infrastructure optimisation'.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Regular data flow mapping. Audit actual data locations against contracted locations. Monitor sub-processor changes.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Contractual data residency requirement. Notification for any infrastructure change affecting data location. Annual data flow audit right.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when data localisation requirements are weak or monitoring is absent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Insurance Coverage Lapse
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider's insurance lapses mid-contract, removing financial backing for claims.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider lets cyber insurance lapse due to premium increases. Data breach occurs 3 months later. Provider lacks financial resources to cover notification costs and regulatory fines.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for ongoing insurance maintenance obligation with annual certificate requirement.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require annual insurance certificates. Immediate notification of lapse. Termination right if not remedied within 30 days.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when insurance is required but no ongoing verification mechanism exists.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Entire Agreement Clause Bypass
contract_threat_patterns.description: Pre-contractual representations used to claim contract terms different from what was signed.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Salesperson makes verbal promises about features and performance. Contract contains entire agreement clause. Customer relies on promises not captured in the contract.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Verify entire agreement clause is present and properly drafted with fraud carve-out.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include robust entire agreement clause. Ensure all negotiated terms are captured in the signed agreement. No reliance on oral representations.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: verify entire agreement clause is present and all oral commitments are documented.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Non-Compete Circumvention Through Affiliates
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider circumvents non-compete restrictions by using affiliate or subsidiary to serve competitor.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider agrees not to serve customer's competitors. Provider's sister company (same parent) takes on competitor's work using shared resources and knowledge.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check if non-compete covers affiliates and related entities. Verify definition of 'competing' is comprehensive.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Extend non-compete to all affiliates. Define 'competitor' broadly. Include information barrier requirements for affiliated entities.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when non-compete does not extend to affiliates or group companies.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Deliberate Service Degradation to Force Customer Exit
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider deliberately degrades service quality to encourage customer to terminate, avoiding provider termination penalties.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider wants to exit unprofitable contract. Instead of terminating (and paying fees), provider reduces staff quality, extends response times, and delivers minimum compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Monitor SLA trends over time. Track personnel changes and quality metrics.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include performance improvement plan obligations. Material and persistent SLA failure = termination for cause by customer (no fees).
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when SLA metrics show declining trend without cause.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Confidential Information Used in AI Model Training
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses customer's confidential data to train AI models, exposing information through model outputs.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider's AI service ingests customer's proprietary threat intelligence data. Data is used to train general model. Competitor queries model and receives customer's proprietary analysis.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review data processing scope for AI training exclusions. Check for model training opt-out provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Explicitly prohibit use of customer data for model training. Require opt-out from all AI training. Include audit right over training data usage.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when AI services process confidential data without training exclusion.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Warranty Voided Through Broad Exclusion
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses overly broad warranty exclusions to deny legitimate warranty claims.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Software has defect. Provider claims warranty is void because customer used non-standard browser version (listed in broad exclusion clause covering 'unsupported configurations').
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review warranty exclusions for breadth. Check if normal usage patterns could trigger exclusions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Limit warranty exclusions to: misuse, unauthorised modification, and force majeure. Exclude normal variations in environment.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when warranty exclusions are broader than reasonable use scenarios.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Termination Notice Procedural Trap
contract_threat_patterns.description: Strict notice requirements used to invalidate legitimate termination attempts.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer sends termination notice by email. Contract requires registered post to specific address. Provider claims notice is invalid and contract auto-renewed for another year.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review notice provisions for: methods, addresses, deemed delivery, and consequences of non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Accept multiple notice methods (email and post). Include deemed delivery rules. Require acknowledgement of receipt.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when notice requirements are unusually strict or limited to single method.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Copyleft Open Source Injection
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider introduces copyleft-licensed open source code into deliverables, contaminating customer's proprietary code.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Developer uses GPL-licensed library in customer's proprietary product. Under GPL, customer must release proprietary source code or cease distribution.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Require SBOM for all deliverables. Run licence compliance scanning on all code deliveries.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require SBOM in SPDX format. Prohibit copyleft without approval. Run automated licence scanning. IP indemnification for contamination.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when software deliverables lack SBOM or open source policy.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Multi-Tenant Data Commingling
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider's multi-tenant architecture fails to properly isolate customer data from other tenants.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Shared database with row-level security. Application bug bypasses tenant isolation. Customer A's confidential data visible to Customer B through API queries.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review data isolation architecture. Check for tenant isolation testing and certification.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require documented isolation architecture. Annual penetration testing of tenant isolation. Per-tenant encryption keys.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when multi-tenant service lacks documented isolation controls.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Regulatory Change Exploitation
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses new regulatory requirements as justification for unilateral contract changes or price increases.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: NIS2 comes into force. Provider claims compliance requires 40% price increase. Customer has no choice but to pay or be non-compliant.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for regulatory change clause with cost allocation mechanism and right to negotiate.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include regulatory change clause: mutual obligation to comply, cost sharing mechanism, right to terminate if costs unreasonable.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when contract lacks regulatory change mechanism for long-term engagements.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Shadow Data Processing Beyond Instructions
contract_threat_patterns.description: Processor processes personal data beyond controller's documented instructions for own purposes.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Cloud provider uses customer's personal data for product analytics and targeted advertising through broadly worded processing instructions.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review processing instructions for specificity. Audit actual processing activities against instructions.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Narrowly define processing instructions. Prohibit processing for provider's own purposes. Include technical controls to prevent unauthorised processing.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when processing instructions are vague or provider has commercial interest in the data.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Data Retention Beyond Stated Purpose
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider retains personal data beyond the period necessary for the stated processing purpose.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contract ends. Provider retains 'anonymised' data indefinitely for analytics. Anonymisation is inadequate and data can be re-identified.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check deletion clause for: timeframe, certification, all copies (including backups), and anonymisation standards.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require deletion within 30 days. Written certification. Deletion of all copies including backups within 90 days. Define anonymisation standard.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when deletion obligations are vague or allow retention of 'anonymised' data without defined standards.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Dispute Resolution Process Exhaustion
contract_threat_patterns.description: Party uses multi-tier dispute resolution as delay tactic to avoid resolving legitimate claims.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer raises material breach. Provider insists on completing all 4 tiers of dispute resolution (negotiation, mediation, expert determination, arbitration) spanning 18 months.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review dispute resolution for: tier timeframes, mandatory vs optional tiers, and emergency/injunctive relief preservation.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Set strict timeframes for each tier: 30 days negotiation, 60 days mediation. Preserve right to seek injunctive relief at any time.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when dispute resolution has no time limits or preserves no emergency relief rights.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Key Personnel Strategic Removal
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider removes key personnel who built the relationship and deep knowledge, replacing with junior staff.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider replaces experienced account manager and technical lead with junior staff 6 months into complex outsourcing. Service quality declines but remains just above SLA threshold.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for key person clause with: named individuals, notice of departure, and replacement approval rights.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Name key personnel. Require 30-day notice of changes. Customer approval for replacements. Knowledge transfer period.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when complex engagement lacks key person provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Indemnification Claim Denial Through Procedural Defect
contract_threat_patterns.description: Indemnifier denies claim based on technical non-compliance with claim procedure.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer notifies provider of IP infringement claim by email instead of registered post. Provider denies indemnification obligation citing procedural non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review indemnification procedure for: notification method, timeframe, and consequences of non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Reasonable notification requirements. Cure period for procedural defects. No forfeiture unless material prejudice from non-compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when indemnification procedure has strict forfeiture provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Security Certification Lapse Without Notification
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider's security certification (ISO 27001, SOC 2) lapses without notifying customer.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider loses ISO 27001 certification after failed surveillance audit. Does not notify customer. Customer's own compliance claims rely on provider's certification.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for certification maintenance obligation with annual evidence and immediate lapse notification.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require ongoing certification maintenance. Immediate notification of lapse or adverse finding. Annual certificate provision. Termination right if not remedied.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when security compliance relies on provider certification without verification mechanism.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Liability Cap Erosion Through Multiple Contracts
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider structures relationship across multiple contracts to reduce effective liability cap per incident.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider splits services into 5 separate contracts each with 12-month fee cap. Incident affects all services but liability is capped per contract, not aggregate relationship.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review if multiple contracts with same provider have separate or aggregate liability caps.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include umbrella liability clause across all agreements with same provider. Aggregate cap across the relationship.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when multiple contracts exist with same provider without aggregate liability mechanism.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Incident Notification Suppression
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider classifies security incidents below notification threshold to avoid disclosure obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider experiences data breach affecting 500 records. Classifies as 'security event' rather than 'incident' to avoid notification obligation. Breach widens to 50,000 records.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review incident classification criteria. Check for obligation to report events below incident threshold.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Define incident broadly. Require notification of all security events (not just incidents). Include audit right over incident logs.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when incident definition is narrow or classification is provider-determined.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Conflicting Governing Law Across Related Agreements
contract_threat_patterns.description: Related agreements governed by different laws creating inconsistent rights and obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: MSA governed by English law. DPA governed by Irish law. Conflicting interpretation of liability for data breach. Each agreement points to different jurisdiction for dispute resolution.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review all related agreements for consistent governing law and dispute resolution.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Align governing law across all related agreements. Single dispute resolution mechanism. Clear hierarchy between agreements.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when related agreements have inconsistent governing law or dispute resolution.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Definition Manipulation to Alter Clause Meaning
contract_threat_patterns.description: Broad or ambiguous definitions used to expand or contract clause scope beyond original intent.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Definition of 'Confidential Information' includes 'information marked as confidential'. Provider never marks anything. Effectively no information is protected.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review all defined terms. Check if definitions are self-limiting or could be manipulated.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Use inclusive definitions: information that a reasonable person would consider confidential, whether or not marked. Include catch-all categories.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when key definitions are narrow or require specific actions (like marking) to activate.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Sanctions Exposure Through Supply Chain
contract_threat_patterns.description: Counter-party's supply chain relationships expose customer to sanctions risk.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider uses sub-processor based in country subject to sectoral sanctions. Customer's data is processed by sanctioned entity. Customer faces secondary sanctions exposure.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review sanctions representations. Require supply chain screening and ongoing monitoring.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include sanctions representation and warranty. Require ongoing screening. Immediate notification of sanctions risk. Termination right for sanctions breach.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when international supply chains lack sanctions screening provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Auto-Renewal Lock-In Trap
contract_threat_patterns.description: Auto-renewal with narrow opt-out window traps customer in unwanted renewal.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Contract auto-renews for 12 months unless 90-day notice given. No renewal reminder sent. Customer discovers renewal after window closes. Locked in for another year.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for auto-renewal provisions: renewal period, notice window, reminder obligation, and price increase mechanism.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require 60-day advance renewal notice from provider. 30-day opt-out window after notice. Cap renewal price increase.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag auto-renewal clauses without provider notification obligations.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Parent Company Guarantee Avoidance
contract_threat_patterns.description: Contract with thinly capitalised subsidiary while parent company avoids guarantee obligations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer contracts with subsidiary with minimal assets. Subsidiary defaults on obligations. Parent company disclaims responsibility. Customer has unrecoverable claim.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Verify contracting entity's financial standing. Check for parent guarantee if entity is subsidiary.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require parent company guarantee. Or require performance bond. Conduct due diligence on contracting entity's financial standing.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when contracting entity is subsidiary without parent guarantee or financial backing.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Confidential Information Disclosure in Legal Proceedings
contract_threat_patterns.description: Counter-party discloses confidential information through legal proceedings or regulatory submissions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: During unrelated litigation, provider discloses customer's confidential information as part of discovery. Information becomes part of public court record.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review confidentiality exceptions for legal proceedings. Check for protective order requirements.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require: prompt notification before disclosure, seek protective order, disclose minimum necessary, and exclude from public filings where possible.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when legal disclosure exception lacks notification and protective order requirements.

contract_threat_patterns.name: BCP Activation Failure
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider's business continuity plan exists on paper but fails when activated due to lack of testing.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Data centre flood. Provider activates BCP. DR site has not been tested in 3 years. Configuration drift means failover fails. RTO of 4 hours becomes 4 days.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for BCP/DR testing obligations with: frequency, scope, customer participation, and results reporting.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require annual DR testing with full failover. Customer observation rights. Results within 10 days. Remediation plan for gaps within 30 days.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when BCP exists but testing requirements are absent or infrequent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Strategic Employee Poaching
contract_threat_patterns.description: Counter-party recruits key personnel, gaining institutional knowledge and disrupting operations.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider recruits customer's lead architect who designed the system the provider maintains. Architect takes institutional knowledge. Provider gains leverage in negotiations.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for mutual non-solicitation clause with: scope, duration, and remedy.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Mutual 12-month non-solicitation for directly involved personnel. Include garden leave payment option. Liquidated damages for breach.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when complex engagements lack non-solicitation protections.

contract_threat_patterns.name: AI-Generated Deliverable IP Uncertainty
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider delivers AI-generated content where IP ownership and originality are legally uncertain.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider uses generative AI to create design documents and code. Deliverables may contain AI-generated content that is not copyrightable. Customer pays for IP it may not legally own.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for AI usage disclosure and IP warranty covering AI-generated content.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require disclosure of AI tool usage. Include IP warranty covering AI-generated content. Indemnification for AI-related IP claims.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when deliverables may include AI-generated content without IP warranty.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Jurisdiction Shopping to Avoid Breach Consequences
contract_threat_patterns.description: Party structures operations across jurisdictions to minimise consequences of contractual breach.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider locates assets in jurisdiction where foreign judgments are difficult to enforce. Provider breaches contract. Customer wins judgment but cannot enforce it.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Assess enforceability of judgments in provider's operational jurisdictions. Check for New York Convention coverage for arbitration.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Use arbitration (New York Convention enforceability). Include multi-jurisdiction enforcement clause. Require assets in enforceable jurisdiction.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when provider's assets are in jurisdictions with limited judgment enforcement.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Supply Chain Cascading Failure
contract_threat_patterns.description: Failure in provider's supply chain cascades to affect customer without contractual protection.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider's cloud infrastructure provider has major outage. Provider's own services fail. Provider claims force majeure citing third-party dependency. No step-in rights.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review provider's critical dependencies. Check if force majeure covers third-party failures. Verify step-in rights.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Exclude provider's supplier failures from force majeure. Require supplier risk management. Include step-in rights. Multi-vendor strategy.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: STRIDE Enumerator: flag when service depends on provider's supply chain without resilience requirements.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Unilateral Service Modification
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider modifies service features, functionality, or architecture without customer consent.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: SaaS provider removes feature that customer depends on as part of 'product evolution'. Terms of service allow unilateral changes. Customer has no remedy.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for service change provisions. Verify customer has consent right for material changes.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require: mutual agreement for material changes, 90-day notice for minor changes, feature freeze for contracted capabilities, and termination right for adverse changes.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when provider reserves right to unilaterally modify service.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Rights Waiver Through Course of Dealing
contract_threat_patterns.description: Party argues that consistent non-enforcement of a right constitutes waiver by conduct.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Customer never claims service credits for 18 months of SLA failures. When claiming credits for major outage, provider argues right was waived by prior non-enforcement.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for no-waiver clause. Verify it requires written waiver.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include clear no-waiver clause: failure to exercise right is not waiver. Waiver must be in writing. Maintain claim log.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when no-waiver clause is absent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Unintended Third-Party Beneficiary Claims
contract_threat_patterns.description: Third parties claim rights under the contract through beneficiary provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: End users of customer's product claim they are third-party beneficiaries of the provider's SLA, bringing direct claims against provider.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for third-party rights exclusion (Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 or equivalent).
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include express exclusion of third-party rights except for intended beneficiaries (e.g., indemnified parties, group companies).
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when third-party rights exclusion is absent.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Shadow IT Procurement Bypassing Contract Controls
contract_threat_patterns.description: Business units engage cloud services directly, bypassing procurement and legal review.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Department signs up for SaaS tool using corporate credit card. Standard terms apply with no DPA, no liability cap, and provider ownership of uploaded data.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review procurement process for shadow IT controls. Check for spend threshold triggers requiring legal review.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Implement approved vendor programme. Require legal review above spend threshold. Centralized SaaS management platform.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Risk Assessor: flag when procurement processes allow unreviewed vendor engagement.

contract_threat_patterns.name: Severability Exploitation to Remove Protective Clauses
contract_threat_patterns.description: Party argues protective clause is unenforceable and should be severed, weakening the other party's position.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Provider argues non-compete clause is unenforceable as restraint of trade. Court severs the clause entirely rather than reducing scope, leaving customer without any protection.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Review severability clause for: blue-pencil provisions, reduction rather than removal, and savings language.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Include blue-pencil clause allowing court to reduce unreasonable terms rather than sever entirely. Step-down provisions.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when severability clause lacks blue-pencil or reduction provisions.

contract_threat_patterns.name: AI Hallucination in Professional Deliverables
contract_threat_patterns.description: Provider uses AI to generate deliverables containing fabricated information (hallucinations) passed off as expert analysis.
contract_threat_patterns.attack_scenario: Legal advisory firm uses LLM to draft compliance analysis. Report cites non-existent regulations and case law. Customer relies on fabricated guidance for business decisions.
contract_threat_patterns.detection: Check for AI usage disclosure requirement and accuracy warranties covering AI-assisted deliverables.
contract_threat_patterns.mitigation: Require: disclosure of AI tool usage, human expert review certification, accuracy warranty, and professional standards compliance.
contract_threat_patterns.agent_use: Contract Reviewer: flag when professional services may use AI without accuracy safeguards.
